subject: Add support to http_proxy & https_proxy buildArgs
commit_message:
Add support to http_proxy & https_proxy buildArgs

This allows to build docker images behind a corporate proxy passing those
values as build arguments in maven.

Change-Id: I0a9d421c5d0fe0fb9957bba30ff020f6fde6f3f9
Signed-off-by: Victor Morales <victor.morales@intel.com>
Issue-Id: AAI-419

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified-1

Build Failed 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/aai-sparky-be-master-verify-java/40/ : FAILURE

No problems were identified. If you know why this problem occurred, please add a suitable Cause for it. ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/aai-sparky-be-master-verify-java/40/ )

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/aai-sparky-be-master-verify-java/40

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Why do you want the proxy setup in the docker image? If the proxy is setup in the host network, the docker images running on the host can access it.

Patch Set 1:

> Why do you want the proxy setup in the docker image? If the proxy
 > is setup in the host network, the docker images running on the host
 > can access it.

AFAIK, the proxy setup in the host is for pulling the images from public repositories but not for building them.  This change is addressing the case when the developer wants to build the image from a corporate network

Patch Set 1:

> > Why do you want the proxy setup in the docker image? If the proxy
 > > is setup in the host network, the docker images running on the
 > host
 > > can access it.
 > 
 > AFAIK, the proxy setup in the host is for pulling the images from
 > public repositories but not for building them.  This change is
 > addressing the case when the developer wants to build the image
 > from a corporate network

Hi Victor,
I understand why you will need a proxy when you are behind a corporate network. What I do not understand is why it is needed for sparky. When you say that "the developer wants to build a image" what do you mean by that. Can you please explain that in detail? I use sparky and I am behind a proxy.

Patch Set 1:

> > > Why do you want the proxy setup in the docker image? If the
 > proxy
 > > > is setup in the host network, the docker images running on the
 > > host
 > > > can access it.
 > >
 > > AFAIK, the proxy setup in the host is for pulling the images from
 > > public repositories but not for building them.  This change is
 > > addressing the case when the developer wants to build the image
 > > from a corporate network
 > 
 > Hi Victor,
 > I understand why you will need a proxy when you are behind a
 > corporate network. What I do not understand is why it is needed for
 > sparky. When you say that "the developer wants to build a image"
 > what do you mean by that. Can you please explain that in detail? I
 > use sparky and I am behind a proxy.

Hey Arul,

What I'm trying to do is to add support to build AAI Images in ONAP on Vagrant, from the end user perspective this operation looks like:

    $ build_docker_image $aai_src_folder/sparky-be

Internally this function is calling a maven instruction (and passing custom proxy values) resulting in the following code

    $ cd /opt/aai/sparky-be/
    $ mvn clean package docker:build  -Ddocker.buildArg.http_proxy=...  -Ddocker.buildArg.https_proxy=...

You said that you're able to build images behind the proxy, I'm wondering how do you do it? Specially for executing the `apt-get update` instruction of the Dockerfile.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

Hi Victor,
Thanks for the explanation. I think I understand now. We run sparky behind a proxy, but the build itself was taken care by Jenkins. So I can see why you will need that change. My concern is if the arguments are not passed for the builds in onap jenkins what would happen. I can see that the ONAP jenkins build also failed for no reason for this change. So try re building the onap build and see if it passes. Because we will need it to pass before the merge happens.
regards
Arul
